_note: The Gym manifest (WP25a): which grammar points act as selectable form categories, grouped into picker columns. 'point' must match the grammar point title exactly (resolved to ids at request time). 'usage' is the plain-language hover explanation; 'example' is a real drill sentence from that point. 'nonstandard': entries hidden behind the 'include non-standard words' toggle — forms that break or sit outside the regular patterns.

entries.label: Present
entries.usage: What someone does now or does regularly. The ending changes with who is doing it.
entries.example: Я читаю книгу. — I am reading a book.

entries.label: Past
entries.usage: What already happened. The ending follows the doer's gender and number, not the person.
entries.example: Она была в Москве. — She was in Moscow.

entries.label: Future (ongoing)
entries.usage: What will be happening — быть + the action, for processes without a finish line.
entries.example: Завтра я буду работать дома. — Tomorrow I will be working at home.

entries.label: Future (completed)
entries.usage: What will get done — one word, present-style endings on the perfective verb.
entries.example: Я позвоню тебе вечером. — I will call you in the evening.

entries.label: Aspect choice
entries.usage: The process verb or the result verb — читать vs прочитать. The heart of the Russian verb.
entries.example: Вчера я наконец прочитал эту книгу. — Yesterday I finally finished reading this book.

entries.label: Commands
entries.usage: Telling someone to do something — informal читай, polite or plural читайте.
entries.example: Скажите, пожалуйста, где метро? — Please tell me, where is the metro?

entries.label: Reflexives (-ся)
entries.usage: Verbs that fold back on their subject — the -ся tail stays through every form.
entries.example: Я занимаюсь русским языком. — I study the Russian language.

entries.label: Would (бы)
entries.usage: Wishes and what-ifs — past form + бы makes 'would'.
entries.example: Я хотел бы чашку кофе. — I would like a cup of coffee.

entries.point: Verbs of motion: идти vs ходить
entries.label: Going on foot
entries.usage: Two different verbs for walking: one trip in progress (идти) vs trips in general (ходить).
entries.example: Сейчас я иду в школу. — I am walking to school right now.

entries.point: Verbs of motion: ехать vs ездить
entries.label: Going by vehicle
entries.usage: Riding or driving splits the same way: one trip (ехать) vs habitual travel (ездить).
entries.example: Сейчас мы едем в Москву. — We are on our way to Moscow now.

entries.point: Prefixed verbs of motion (прийти, уйти, выйти)
entries.label: Arriving & leaving
entries.usage: Prefixes aim the motion — при- arrives, у- departs, вы- exits — and flip the aspect.
entries.example: Он пришёл домой в семь часов. — He came home at seven o'clock.

entries.label: Prepositional (where)
entries.usage: Where something is — after в and на for locations, the noun's ending changes.
entries.example: Книга на столе. — The book is on the table.

entries.label: Accusative (what)
entries.usage: The thing the action lands on — feminine -а becomes -у, masculine things stay put.
entries.example: Я читаю книгу. — I am reading a book.

entries.label: Accusative (whom)
entries.usage: When the object is alive, masculine nouns borrow the genitive ending instead.
entries.example: Я вижу брата каждый день. — I see my brother every day.

entries.label: Genitive (whose)
entries.usage: Ownership and 'of' — the possessor follows the thing possessed, in genitive.
entries.example: Это машина брата. — This is my brother's car.

entries.label: Genitive (there's no…)
entries.usage: What's missing — нет always pulls the missing thing into the genitive.
entries.example: У меня нет времени. — I have no time.

entries.label: Dative (to whom)
entries.usage: The receiver — whoever something is given, said, or shown to takes the dative.
entries.example: Я даю брату книгу. — I am giving my brother a book.

entries.label: Instrumental (with what)
entries.usage: The tool or companion — writing with a pen, tea with sugar, going with a friend.
entries.example: Я пишу ручкой. — I write with a pen.

entries.label: Counting forms
entries.usage: Numbers boss the noun around: 2–4 take one form, 5+ another — час, часа, часов.
entries.example: Мне двадцать два года. — I am twenty-two years old.

entries.label: Agreement
entries.usage: The adjective mirrors its noun — новый дом, новая книга, новое слово.
entries.example: Это новый дом. — This is a new house.

entries.label: Comparatives
entries.usage: More and -er — usually -ее on the stem, with a few classics like лучше and больше.
entries.example: Эта книга интереснее, чем фильм. — This book is more interesting than the film.

entries.label: Superlatives
entries.usage: The most — самый in front, agreeing with the noun like any adjective.
entries.example: Москва — самый большой город России. — Moscow is the biggest city in Russia.

entries.label: Short forms
entries.usage: A clipped predicate form — рад, рада, рады — only after 'to be', never before a noun.
entries.example: Я очень рад тебя видеть. — I am very glad to see you.

entries.label: My / your / our
entries.usage: Possessives agree like adjectives — мой дом, моя книга, моё окно.
entries.example: Это моя книга. — This is my book.

entries.label: Свой (one's own)
entries.usage: The boomerang possessive — points back at the subject, whoever that is.
entries.example: Я люблю свою работу. — I love my (own) job.
